title: In war, anything can happen; in investing, only management matters. 6 stocks from different sectors with upside potential of up to 27%
summary: Net margin is a useful metric. It tells you how much profit remains after costs, interest, and tax are deducted from revenue. But it does not tell you whether the sector is structurally tough. It does not tell you whether management has pricing discipline. It does not tell you whether the company has survived a crisis that killed competitors. It does not tell you whether the business can reinvest. It does not tell you whether low margin is a weakness or simply the price of operating in a hard but defensible industry. It does not necessarily tell you whether the stock is worth picking up in the market.

title: In a volatile market, odds do favour contrarians, but only if they have an edge: 4 stocks from different sectors with dividend yield of up to 5.8%
summary: When a fundamentally strong business is available at 4% dividend yield, the probability that you will have both dividend and the capital gains, rather than just one or the other, is materially higher than what the market is currently pricing. You are not betting on a recovery that might not come. You are betting on a recovery that, given the quality of the business and the management’s track record, has the probability skewed in your favour. In a market oscillating violently, where growth is scarce and volatility is high, that probability edge is worth looking at.
